selection_reasons.choose-falsify-9x3-full-bank: current panel winner retained unchanged for replication
selection_reasons.repair-falsify-5x3-efficient: pooled champion across 21 fresh panels: 113/270 exact; 40.0% weakest family

source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 24 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged. Its parallel falsifying repair panel remains the principal replicated control for answer-generating review.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Test candidate-only selection on the high-coverage nine-answer bank. Three independent falsifying selectors see up to eight shuffled unique candidates and cannot synthesize replacements, isolating whether a selection panel can close the oracle-to-final gap without repair variance.
strategies.name: Nine-Bank Falsifying Selection Panel

strategies.hypothesis: Ablate selector plurality while preserving the successful candidate-only constraint. If one falsifying selector approaches the three-selector panel, the selection architecture—not reviewer voting—is the main gain and two calls per case can be removed.
strategies.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection

strategies.hypothesis: Preserve the frozen candidate-generation pipeline unchanged. Its repeated introduction of correct answers absent from the base bank tests the orthogonal hypothesis that blind diversified construction can lift oracle coverage, while the terminal integrator measures whether those gains can be converted safely.
strategies.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration
